title: Creamy Peppery Japanese Brie Broth
description: A silky broth that marries the rich depth of brie with spicy Szechuan peppercorns and a touch of Japanese umami, perfect for a romantic date night.
instructions: Heat the vegetable broth in a medium saucepan over medium heat until it just begins to simmer.
Add the sesame oil, rice vinegar, crushed Szechuan peppercorns, and red pepper flakes to the broth and stir to combine.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, letting the mixture simmer for 2 minutes to release their aromas.
Add the cubed brie to the pot, stirring gently until the cheese is melted and the broth becomes silky and creamy.
If using, stir in the torn seaweed sheets and let the broth simmer for another 1‑2 minutes; they will add a subtle umami depth.
Taste the broth and adjust seasoning with a pinch more pepper or vinegar if desired—remember this is meant to be spicy and bright.
Remove the pot from heat and ladle the broth into two warmed bowls.
Garnish each bowl with sliced green onions and a few extra peppercorns if desired, then serve immediately.
The broth is best enjoyed hot, paired with a light side such as steamed rice or a simple miso-free noodle dish.
